text: SD Video Digitisation HD Video Digtitisation Sound Digitisation Our heritage HD (high definition) video tape digitisation service support clients with audio-visual video tape digitisation from 1 video tape to 1000's. Based around World Leading AWS conversion and Blackmagic units we offer archival preservation video outputs to include 10-bit uncompressed, FFV1 lossless for the recommended quality outputs in line with iASA Industry Standards. To include all Pro-Res outputs, Quicktime .MOV video files. The purpose of a preservation copy is to create a high quality duplicate of the original tape. This way, if the original deteriorates beyond use the preservation copy will be able to take its place. Video preservation copies generally have the following characteristics: High resolution, High bitrate, No or very little compression, Open or non-proprietary formats. The purpose of an access copy is to create a copy of your record that you can play back easily. This can be a DVD or mp4 file to allow play back on a computer or DVD player. Video access copies generally have the following characteristics: May be reduced resolutions, Low bitrate, Fairly compressed. Files may be delivered on hard drive, flash drive or optical disk; whichever method you select you should be prepared to make a second, or backup, copy to help ensure the safety of your records.

text: SD Video Digitisation HD Video Digtitisation Sound Digitisation Our heritage sound audio tape digitisation service support clients with audio-visual tape digitisation from 1 audio tape to 1000's. Based around World Leading Apogee and Izotope units we offer archival preservation audio outputs to include Waveform Audio File Format (WAV, WAVE, Broadcast BWF) AIFF, MP3 or CD in formats 16-bit 44.1 kHz (CD) upto 24-bit 192 kHz for the recommended quality outputs in line with iASA Industry Standards. The purpose of a preservation copy is to create a high quality duplicate of the original tape. This way, if the original deteriorates beyond use the preservation copy will be able to take its place. Video preservation copies generally have the following characteristics: High resolution, High bitrate, No or very little compression, Open or non-proprietary formats. The purpose of an access copy is to create a copy of your audio that you can play back easily. This can be a CD, WAV or mp3 file to allow play back on a computer or CD player. Audio access copies generally have the following characteristics: May be reduced resolutions, Low bitrate, Fairly compressed. Files may be delivered on hard drive, flash drive or optical disk; whichever method you select you should be prepared to make a second, or backup, copy to help ensure the safety of your records. Clients include Langham Archive https://www.allsouls.org / and Walter Hayes Archive https://walterhayes.co.uk/acknowledgments

text: Every family has precious memories which are on obsolete tapes that require digitising to either DVD or digital MPEG4 files. It is important to realise tape is deteriorating, and with an expected age of around 15-20 years, it is even more important for your family media to be converted sooner rather than later so you can watch your favourite home movies again. Our studio specialises in converting family media, from old VHS tapes, camcorder tapes, video cassettes to DVD and MPEG4 digital files. IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS - HOW TO ORDER 1. Add all of your items together and either post or bring your order in to our office. 2. If posting, please ensure a letter is within the box, to include your full name, address, email and mobile and post using a tracked service to: Oxford Duplication Centre Ltd 29 Banbury Road Kidlington Oxfordshire OX5 1AQ Our offices are open Monday to Thursday 8am-4pm, Friday 8am-3pm. Tel: 01865 457000 or email: cheryl@oxfordduplicationcentre.com 3. Once received, we will prepare an estimate for your approval by email. Once approved, your order will be completed within 15 working days. Urgent orders can be supported but may incur an additional cost. text: Oxford Duplication provide a multi-award winning video tape and audio tape digitisation and preservation service which supports clients in the conversion of discontinued magnetic media formats of valuable tape and audio records and archives from 1 tape to 1000's. Our engineers and technicians have a wealth of experience in the digitisation of analogue and digital magnetic tape. Working with archives, museums, universities and consumer clients, we can carefully repair, convert and restore magnetic tape to high quality digital files. Using some of the world's leading AWS Elemental, Blackmagic Decks, Prism Sound and Izotope audio-visual equipment and conversion systems, our transfers are digitised to the highest quality, ensuring preservation of magnetic analogue media to digital outputs adhering to the preservation of digital records using The National Archives Standards and The International Association of Sound and Audiovisual Archives TC-06 guidelines. section: Benefits of Heritage Digitisation by Preserving through Scanning
text: The digitisation of heritage and historical archives ensures that not only the past is preserved but that the media presented for media can be retained, preserved and viewed with mimimum damage. Digitisation is important to convert physical collections into digital formats, enabling them to be accessible to a wider audience who can explore and connect with the past anywhere throughout the world. Creating digital copies, especially fragile items will mitigate the risk of damage from handling and environmental factors In the tragic event of a disaster, such as fire or flood, digital copies can provide an invaluable backup.

text: Our specialist document and manuscript digitisation service digitises historical documents into National Archive Standard digital formats. Perfect for the preservation of parchments, registers, manuscripts, letters, and archival papers, easily making them accessible for online viewing. Our flatbed scanning machinery can carefully digitise fragile books, printed documents, war diaries, old newspapers, parchment curled or straight and deed packs. Media is carefully relaxed and digitised to produce an exact digital replica of the image. Using National Archive Standards, you can trust our expertise in preparing the highest quality digital outputs required for future preservation. Our award-winning photographic film digitisation process for all film types including medium format, transparencies, 35mm, aperture cards, microfilms, glass plates and specialist film types is always an extemely popular department. Our archive technicians use specialist scanners capable of digitising imagery in the highest quality RAW or TIFF outputs.

text: What is an ephemera collection? Ephemera are often paper-based, printed items, including menus, ticket stubs, newspapers, postcards, posters, sheet music, stickers, and greeting cards. Ephemera is a term for items that are meant to be used temporarily and then discarded, but have since become collectibles. Ephemera can be digitised in a number of ways, including: The Jam Collection: This vast private collection of printed ephemera is being digitised by our company to be made available online. The collection includes posters, documents and high-resolution color images. The collection is a resource for researchers studying the history of this popular music culture. The Wandsworth Museum: This project digitises source materials related to events and links them together in a digital archive. The project works with local institutions to preserve the heritage of the local communities. The Kidlington and District Historical Society: This incredible collection of over 60,000 documents, tickets, posters, diskettes was digitised by our company and comprises one of the largest village empherera collections in the UK. This resource will be made available online to preserve and provide researchers valuable historical information dating back to the village's earliest beginnings. https://www.kidlingtonhistory.org.uk/ Digitisation and Archiving Specialists: Family Archives (paper, video, cine and photographs) | Government and Corporate Documents | Photographic Film Materials | Paper & Parchment | Books & Scrapbooks | Digital Media | Audio, Video & Cine Motion Picture Film

section: Scanning and Caring for Private and Family Collection | Preserving Your Family Archives
text: What is a Digital Family Archive? A digital archive is your personal historical documents and objects that provide evidence of the past which have been digitised and made available online. This is the best protection for your private collection. Creating a Digital Archive of Your Belongings. Many clients have collections which are personally significant materials. These may include diaries and letters handed down through generations or photographs of significant life events or even a set of old tickets stubs. These paper-based collections deteriorate over time, but making small changes in storage and handling can have a huge impact on the lifespan of the materials. What formats are best for archiving files? Technology has become more secure ensuring online media is universally accessible. For images, we digitise using TIFF or JPEG format. For documents, we offer PDF format which can be opened regardless of software or operating system. For video files, we suggest MPEG4. section: Working in All Industry Sectors Digitising All Document Types
text: Data Protection: ICO | NHS Digital | General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Preparation Process NHS & Healthcare | Pharmaceutical | Local Government | Libraries & Museums | Educational Sectors | Engineering & Nuclear | Heritage Trusts | Audio-Visual Production Houses | Automotive | Charities | Housing Associations | Private Archives | Consumer Clients STATE OF THE ART CONVERSION FACILITIES IN OXFORD: With highly skilled technicians, working from our large conversion facility in Kidlington, 5 miles from the beautiful city of Oxford, we have extensive experience in helping companies digitise in all areas of archiving documents, microfilm, and more from 1 box to large archive collections and bulk scanning projects. SCANNING AND CONVERTING ALL DOCUMENT TYPES: Our experienced team will carefully prepare your paper documents, of any size, removing first staples and other media prior to digitisation. We can provide on-demand, back file and on-going (daily, weekly, monthly) scanning services based on your specific requirements. Our scanners have the capacity to scan thousands of pages of paper daily, transferring information from a large bundle of paper to digital, typically as PDF, PDF/OCR, PDF/A, TIFF or JPEG files. PDF/OCR (SEARCHABLE CONTENT) FOR ON-DEMAND RETRIEVAL: Once the documents are processed the output digital files will be high quality searchable PDFs with OCR (searchable content). As the process starts, paper documents are converted into digital formats for ease of storage, search, retrieval, and use. Scanning software with optical character recognition capabilities process the image files and pull out the required information. INDEXING - TAKING IT ONE STEP FURTHER: Indexing software optimises text for search by identifying and categorising documents and applying further search criteria to obtain an accurate result. Our services will create and provide a full managed and independant index of your PDF document archive. This embeds an index of the words in the document. Utilising Adobe Acrobat you search the index of documents. FILE DELIVERY OPTIONS: Once digitised, your digital files will be securely stored on our ShareFile Encrypted sever or in your local server, hard drive or own facility which will allow you to access and retrieve them and download securely.

text: Our professional multi-award winning fragile paper digitisation services support corporate, heritage and consumer clients, digitising fragile collections for preservation, online use or reprinting. Using high-quality Repro-Camera systems, Book-Eye scanners and contactless scanners which convert upto A1 in size, with archival scan technology for handling any project volume size, will can ensure even the most fragile papers remain safe during the digitisation process. Once digitised, we can offer options for digital preservation to include, searchable PDF indexing of archives, accessing specific items of interest online, preservation of damage-pront items and maintaining a record of history. Bound Volumes Newspapers Registrars and Ledgers Laboratory Books Diary and Magazines Photograph Albums and Scrapbooks Photographs Parchment and Manuscripts Personnel Records and Reports Published Books Index Cards Bulk Loose Documents Oversized Maps Engineering Plans Artwork Archive Collections | Fragile Papers Digital output include Archival TIFF/RAW, JPEG, PDF (PDFA) and PDF OCR (searchable content), multipage PDFs, WORD or eBooks. Delivery can be via hard drive, electronic download or secure FTP.

text: Our multi-award winning studio is highly recommended for the preservation of art prints, parchment and rare maps, to include other mediums that require high quality digital solutions, for online and archival use. Offering output formats of your choice, typically TIFF and JPEG, we can support projects from 1 piece of artwork to the many thousands. Taking scanning even further, our large format A0+ scanner is designed specifically to scan fine art enabling contact free scanning of works of art like oil and acrylic paintings, watercolours, charcoal and pastel drawings, mixed media collages, and antique works of art; at 600dpi in formats up to 914mm x 1524mm / 36 x 60 inches. This machine is designed for archiving and reproduction of works of art. Original artwork is moved under the CCD camera elements completely contact free, protecting the art from any damage and making it possible to scan a painting that is still wet, as long as it is framed or the sides are dry enough to place the artwork on the scanning bed.

text: Our professional multi-award winning book scanning services support corporate, heritage and consumer clients, digitising book collections for preservation, online use or reprinting. text: For over 15 years, our multi-award winning specialist team of archive technicians and audio-visual engineers have been working for production houses, museums, local government, libraries, corporate and private clients offering audio-visual, photographic film and document digitisation and imaging services in line with IASA guidelines and The National Archives Standards for analogue media including rare, fragile and specialist collections. For over 15 years, our multiaward winning specialist team of archive technicians and audio-visual engineers have been working for production houses, museums, local government, libraries, corporate and private clients offering audio-visual, photographic film and document digitisation and imaging services in line with IASA guidelines and The National Archives Standards for analogue media including rare, fragile and specialist collections. We are proud to be one of the leading archiving, digitisation and imaging companies in the UK and Europe offering cutting edge quality audio-visual conversions using World leading AWS Elemental archival analogue conversion units, alongside leading Blackmagic decks, Prism Sound and Izotope audio restoration. Alongside our highly impressive archive department offering Bookeye, Contex, Silverfast AI Archive Suite and Adobe Creative Suite software to provide all supported digital imaging output formats. Our state of the art in-house facilities in Kidlington, just outside the beautiful City of Oxford and within easy reach of all transport links. With a large car park and lovely gardens, you are welcome to drop orders in during our office hours. To ensure your confidence, we adhere to the preservation of digital records using The National Archives Standards and The International Association of Sound and Audiovisual Archives TC-06 guidelines . We actively support The National Archives in tackling the challenges of digital preservation, ensuring continued access to digital information in the future.

text: Quick Price List Contact Us Our services support clients with professional microfilm, aperture card and microfiche scanning services to all digital output services to include PDF, PDF/A, PDF/OCR, TIFF and JPEG options. Suporting you with the digitisation of all your microfilm archives. â¢ Our services digitise Microfilm, Microfiche and Aperture Cards â¢ Corporate & Archival Output Formats - TIFF & PDF â¢ Fast turnaround time â¢ Fully compliant to data security and confidentiality standards The process of scanning and converting microfilm, microfiche and apertrure cards are digitised using state-of-the-art film scanners, capable of digitising upto 1800dpi. Standard film for bulk orders are typically digitised as PDF 300dpi, but working with archives and museums, we are capable of converting film in uncompressed TIFF formats. Once digitised, the multipage or single page PDF's are then converted using OCR (optical character recognition), enabling clients to have fully searchable and extractable text from the digital images. Digital output can prepared to our secure FileShare portal, hard drive, USB or clients own FTP systems. text: Oxford Duplication Centre provide ad-hoc and one-off shredding services in Oxford for companies and private clients who do not require a regular collection service. These services are perfect if you are moving offices and clearing out documentation that is no longer required. Or for consumer clients working through old archive documents that are not required at the end of each year. Our services are award winning. With our one-off shredding service you can bring your documents in during our office hours and leave them with our team. Once your documents are shredded, we will issue you with our 'proof of shredding' certificate for your digital records. CERTIFICATE OF REGISTRATION NO: CBDU540973 All shredding is prepared at our offices with a certificate of completion. With the law it states through The Data Protection Act 1998 that all materials relating to individuals, employees, customers and company information such as suppliers accounts must be disposed of in a secure manner. We can ensure maximum protection with our on-site distruction services. Please contact cheryl@oxfordduplicationcentre.com or call 01865 457000 and speak to one of our friendly technicians. section: Film Type Deterioration | Why its important to digitise now!
text: All formats of film types deteriorate due to either age or fluctuating temperatures; which include leaving film in garages, lofts or basements. Once the deterioration process starts, you will notice stipling and curling on the film types, blemishes which look like dirt spots or spider veins. WATER, FIRE DAMAGED, BENT, WARPED OR SCRATCHED EMULSION DAMAGED, DIRTY, MOLDY, FADED, DARK, POOR COLOUR, EXPOSURE OR OLD SLIDES. For consumer and publication film types, we use a combination of techniques to remove surface defects (iSRD) before reconstructing the colour (ACR). The final process is grain equilisation management. The software removes surface defects and restores colour to faded slides. Each machine adheres to The National Archives Standards, ensuring focus, colour management and lighting are automatically calibrated. We run each image through Silverfast Archive iSRD. The ACR Adaptive Color Restoration, refreshes faded colors and normalises oversaturated colours The programe removes most of the dust and film scratches, correct for the effects of film grain, and restore faded color and correct shading in shadow areas of the image. Archival photographic film types are prepared TIFF RAW for heritage preservation. Additional asset JPEG files can be prepared with the above restoration processes. Future Proofing your Corporate, Heritage and Consumer Archives We provide high quality digital imaging of heritage, archive and research collections, helping preserve originals through sharing material through digital access Our capabilities include: High resolution digital files of audio-visual, photographic film and paper/heritage based media. Preservation capture methods for larger collections. Capabilities for digitising a variety of formats. Optical Character Recognition (OCR). Experienced team providing solutions for your collection. JPEG10 4800dpi Compressed Family use for viewing on all devices SRDx (scratch and defect removal) | colour correction | hand cleaned File guide size 8 - 10MB Price List TIFF/RAW 3200dpi Uncompressed Publication use but manageable file size iSRD (infrared scratch and removal) | histograph colour management | hand cleaned File guide size 108MB Price List TIFF or RAW 4800dpi Uncompressed National Archive Standard digital files National Archival Standards processing to client specific specification File guide size 165MB Price List WHAT IS RAW? RAW files are uncompressed and unprocessed image data. The scanned images contain all the information from the analogue originals that our scanners were able to capture. This includes the data of your scanner's infrared channel.

text: A digital archive is your personal historical documents and objects that provide evidence of the past which have been digitised and made available online. This is the best protection for your private collection. Digital output include Archival TIFF/RAW, JPEG, PDF (PDFA) and PDF OCR (searchable content), multipage PDFs, WORD or eBooks. Delivery can be via hard drive, electronic download or secure FTP. Our team of experts can advise on all aspects of archive digitisation, from preparing original records for imaging (including their conservation and safety) to data and storage. We help individuals, archives, businesses, and private organisations maximize the potential of their collections through digitisation. Recognising the uniqueness of each collection, our team will work with you to create a customised service that meets your needs. section: Film Type Deterioration | Why its important to digitise now!
text: All formats of film types deteriorate due to either age or fluctuating temperatures; which include leaving film in garages, lofts or basements. Once the deterioration process starts, you will notice stipling and curling on the film types, blemishes which look like dirt spots or spider veins. WATER, FIRE DAMAGED, BENT, WARPED OR SCRATCHED EMULSION DAMAGED, DIRTY, MOLDY, FADED, DARK, POOR COLOUR, EXPOSURE OR OLD SLIDES. For consumer and publication film types, we use a combination of techniques to remove surface defects (iSRD) before reconstructing the colour (ACR). The final process is grain equilisation management. The software removes surface defects and restores colour to faded slides. Each machine adheres to The National Archives Standards, ensuring focus, colour management and lighting are automatically calibrated. We run each image through Silverfast Archive iSRD. The ACR Adaptive Color Restoration, refreshes faded colors and normalises oversaturated colours The programe removes most of the dust and film scratches, correct for the effects of film grain, and restore faded color and correct shading in shadow areas of the image. Archival photographic film types are prepared TIFF RAW for heritage preservation. Additional asset JPEG files can be prepared with the above restoration processes. Safety Film Acetate Film Nitrate Film

JPEGs use smart compression to make file sizes smaller. This compression is heavier in color than clarity, making it perfect for consumer and general viewing. WHAT IS A TIFF? TIFFs are lossless compression files . Resulting in high quality large file images. When a TIFF image is edited and saved, no loss of data occurs, making this format perfect for publication and archives. WHAT IS RAW? RAW files are uncompressed and unprocessed image data. The scanned images contain all the information from the analogue originals that our scanners were able to capture. This includes the data of your scanner's infrared channel. text: Our multi-award winning professional technicians can digitise high-quality images of large format drawings and plans to digital formats TIFF, PDF and JPEG. Using National Archive Standard large format scanning software, we can produce high image resolution and at original scale with OCR (optical character recognition) as an additional extra. Heritage and delicate wide format documents and media require careful delicate digitisation, which should never be subjected to the feed-through scanners because of the fragile nature. Our professional technicians use specialised flatbed scanning technology, careful handling and ensure a 'once only scan' to preserve the original material. Each scan will capture detail in high resolution to The National Archive Specifications. Our services are able to detect and digitise the most faint marks, capturing all detail to TIFF, JPEG, PDF upto 1200dpi in full colour managed outputs.

text: Large format scanner designed specifically to scan fine art WideTEK 36ART enables contact free scanning of works of art like oil and acrylic paintings, watercolours, charcoal and pastel drawings, mixed media collages, and antique works of art; at 600dpi in formats up to 914mm x 1524mm / 36 x 60 inches. Our specialist scanner is designed for archiving and reproduction of works of art. Original media is moved under the CCD camera elements completely contact free, protecting the art from any damage and making it possible to scan media that is still wet, as long as it is framed or the sides are dry enough to place the artwork on the scanning bed. Contact cheryl on 01865 457000 cheryl@oxfordduplicationcentre.com for more information. text: Contact Us Heritage Blog Oxford Duplication offer specialised digitisation services to support digital preservation for Government, museum and heritage collections, ensuring National Archive Standard high-quality digitisation and preservation, ensuring the capture of digital records for long-term safeguarding.
